assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met |'. Acceptance Criteria Detail table (lines 24-29) shows all 5 criteria as PASS: (1) single license filter via validate_license_param and IN filter, (2) comma-separated filter via split(',') and is_in(), (3) 400 validation via Expression::parse rejection to AppError::BadRequest, (4) pagination integration with filter applied before count/fetch, (5) unchanged response shape with PaginatedResults<PackageSummary>. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each conclude with 'Verdict: PASS'.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: '| Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files match the task spec exactly: 2 modified (`list.rs`, `service/mod.rs`), 1 created (`tests/api/package.rs`). No out-of-scope files, no unimplemented files. |'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on the PR |'. report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate |'. No sub-task creation evidence exists in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/report files: report.md (verification report) and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md (per-criterion analysis). No code files (*.rs, *.toml, etc.) are present in outputs. The report text exclusively describes reading and analyzing the diff — e.g., criterion-1.md references 'lines 25-33 of diff' and 'lines 89-105 of diff' as read-only analysis, never indicating modification.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with '### Overall: PASS' followed by a summary paragraph and detailed tables presenting findings. There is no mention of merging, approving, or taking any merge action. The footer states 'This report was AI-generated by [sdlc-workflow/verify-pr]' indicating it is a report for review, not an automated merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines. Changes are limited to query parameter handling, database filtering, and test code. |'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test changes are in a new file (`tests/api/package.rs`); 4 new test functions added, 0 modified, 0 deleted |'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all required verdicts: Scope Containment (line 7, PASS), Diff Size (line 8, PASS), Commit Traceability (line 9, N/A) from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns (line 10, PASS) from Security; CI Status (line 11, PASS), Acceptance Criteria (line 12, PASS), Verification Commands (line 15, N/A) from Correctness; Test Quality (line 13, PASS) and Test Change Classification (line 14, ADDITIVE) from Style/Conventions. Test Quality explicitly combines all three sub-verdicts: 'All 4 test functions have documentation comments' (Test Documentation), 'No repetitive tests detected' (Repetitive Test Detection), and 'Eval Quality: N/A' (Eval Quality).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: Scope Containment row provides file-by-file comparison ('2 modified (`list.rs`, `service/mod.rs`), 1 created (`tests/api/package.rs`)') and Files Changed section (lines 40-46) lists each file with action and purpose. Security: Sensitive Patterns row describes what was scanned ('added lines'), what patterns were searched ('secrets, credentials, API keys, sensitive patterns'), and characterizes the code content ('query parameter handling, database filtering, and test code'). Correctness: Acceptance Criteria Detail section (lines 23-29) provides per-criterion code-level verification referencing specific functions and code patterns; criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide extensive code-level analysis with line references. Style/Conventions: Test Quality row details test documentation ('All 4 test functions have documentation comments') and repetitive test detection ('each test covers a distinct behavior (single filter, multi filter, invalid input, pagination)'); Test Requirements Detail section (lines 31-38) maps each test requirement to a specific test function. The report clearly goes well beyond pass/fail verdicts.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13, Test Quality row: 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews present).' Test Quality is PASS despite Eval Quality being N/A, confirming Eval Quality does not negatively affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A per report.md line 13: 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews present).' Root-Cause Investigation row (line 6) confirms: 'No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate.' No sub-task creation evidence exists in any output file.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on the PR |' — determined by absence of review comments, not domain analysis. Line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate |' — determined by absence of sub-tasks, not domain analysis. Both appear as the first two rows in the summary table (lines 5-6), preceding the domain-specific verdicts (Scope Containment onward at line 7), consistent with orchestrator-level determination.
